records.ety: [AS. under under + m<?/l part or portion; cf. AS. underm<?/l midday. See Under, Meal a part, and cf. Undern.]

defs: The inferior, or after, part of the day; the afternoon.
Hence, something occurring or done in the afternoon; esp., an afternoon meal; supper; also, an afternoon nap; a siesta.
quotes: Another great supper, or undermeal, was made ready for them, coming home from ditching and plowing.
I think I am furnished with Cattern [Catharine] pears for one undermeal.
In a narrower limit than the forty years' undermeal of the seven sleepers.
